hypothesis: If graph neural network training on variable-size graphs uses dynamic batching that adaptively sets padding targets, then the mean gradient-update step time on a single A100 GPU at matched batch sizes will be lower than with static-constant, static-2N, or static-64 padding across QM9 and AFLOW using SchNet, PaiNN, and MPEU because dynamic batching minimizes over-padding and its recompilation overhead is amortized over long runs.
